root_text.sn45.25:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco micchādiṭṭhiko hoti, micchāsaṅkappo, micchāvāco, micchākammanto, micchāājīvo, micchāvāyāmo, micchāsati, micchāsamādhi—
root_text.sn45.25:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sammādiṭṭhiko hoti, sammāsaṅkappo, sammāvāco, sammākammanto, sammāājīvo, sammāvāyāmo, sammāsati, sammāsamādhi—

translation_text.sn45.25:1.2: “Mendicants, I will teach you an untrue person and a true person.
translation_text.sn45.25:1.4: And what is an untrue person?
translation_text.sn45.25:1.5: It’s someone who has wrong view, wrong thought, wrong speech, wrong action, wrong livelihood, wrong effort, wrong mindfulness, and wrong immersion.
translation_text.sn45.25:1.6: This is called an untrue person.
translation_text.sn45.25:2.1: And what is a true person?
translation_text.sn45.25:2.2: It’s someone who has right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.25:2.3: This is called a true person.”
